commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fce3df084a5acaa324f799d583c43075f271a8fc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an `openclaw sessions compact <key>` CLI wrapper for `sessions.compact`, rejects CLI `/compact` messages with exit 1, adds tests, and documents the command/RPC.

PR surface: Source +185, Tests +123, Docs +54. Total +362 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review finding: source inspection shows sibling `sessions` subcommands merge parent options, while the new compact action forwards only child options. I did not run the CLI because this review had to keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are contributor real-behavior proof plus a focused code fix; automation should not take over while the external proof gate is still unmet.

target.title: feat(cli): add `openclaw sessions compact` and fail loudly on CLI `/compact` (fixes #90640)
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90640.

`sessions.compact` was reachable only as an internal Gateway RPC: there was no
CLI command and no docs, and `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` silently
no-opped with exit 0 (the slash-command handler rejects CLI-originated senders,
so the message fell through to an ordinary agent turn that compacted nothing).

This PR closes both gaps:

- Adds `openclaw sessions compact <key>` as a first-class command wrapping the
  existing `sessions.compact` RPC. It exits non-zero whenever the gateway
  reports a failed compaction — both a transport error and an `ok:false`
  payload — so automation never mistakes a silent no-op for success.
- Makes `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` fail loudly: it prints a redirect
  to `openclaw sessions compact <key>` and exits 1 instead of silently exiting
  0. The shared chat-side `/compact` handler is left untouched, so chat
  behaviour and message delivery are unchanged.
- Strictly validates `--max-lines` and `--timeout` (positive integers only).
- Documents the command and the `sessions.compact` RPC in docs/cli/sessions.md.

## Why this is safe (vs. touching the shared handler)

The fix is confined to the CLI `--message` dispatch path. It does **not** modify
the shared `commands-compact.ts` handler that powers chat `/compact`, so there
is no compatibility or message-delivery blast radius — only the CLI path that
was already broken changes behaviour.

## Proof

**Real behavior proof**: A live RPC-over-WebSocket run starts the real Gateway
server, seeds a 500-line transcript on disk, calls the production
`sessions.compact` RPC with `maxLines: 50`, and reads back both the truncated
JSONL and the `.bak` archive across the filesystem boundary.

**Before**: the seeded transcript is 500 lines on disk.

**After**: the transcript is truncated to 50 lines on disk and the original 500
lines are preserved verbatim in a sibling `.bak` archive.

```
rpc sessions.compact { key: "agent:main:main", maxLines: 50 }
response: {"ok":true,"key":"agent:main:main","compacted":true,
           "archived":"<tmp>/proof-sess-90640.jsonl.bak.<ts>","kept":50}

transcript lines BEFORE compact (on disk): 500
transcript lines AFTER  compact (on disk): 50
archived .bak lines (original preserved):  500
```

**CLI exit codes**: `openclaw sessions compact` exits non-zero on a transport
error and on an `ok:false` payload; `openclaw agent --message '/compact'` now
exits 1 with a redirect to `openclaw sessions compact <key>` instead of a silent
exit 0. Verified by 11 unit tests against the real command functions (`Test
Files 2 passed (2)`, `Tests 11 passed`).

## Tests

- `src/commands/sessions-compact.test.ts` — 5 unit tests: success token delta,
  `ok:false` → exit 1, RPC throw → exit 1, JSON-mode `ok:false` → payload +
  exit 1, params forwarding (`agentId`/`maxLines`).
- `src/commands/agent-via-gateway.test.ts` — parametrized cases proving
  `/compact`, `/compact <text>`, `/compact:<text>`, `/COMPACT`, and
  `  /Compact  ` are all rejected with exit 1 before any gateway or embedded
  turn, while `/compacting …` is NOT mistaken for a control command.
target.author: alix-007
